title: Metastability and clock-domain crossing
description: A flop sampling an async signal near its clock edge can settle to an undefined value; synchronizers reduce but never eliminate the probability.
body: Use when any signal crosses between unrelated clocks. When setup/hold is violated, the flop output can be metastable and resolve unpredictably; a 2-flop synchronizer gives the first flop time to settle, lowering failure probability (raising MTBF) but never to zero. Multi-bit buses need gray coding or async FIFOs because bits can resolve independently. Do not treat a single synchronizer as making CDC 'safe'. Actions: classify each crossing and apply the right structure plus constraints. Validation signals include a CDC tool report and MTBF estimate. Risk boundary: CDC bugs appear only in the field and intermittently; never dismiss a CDC warning.
